name: bridge_tool_segments_twist
description: mesh.bridgeTool (task 0357) — Segments/Twist/Remove Polygons golden. Base fixture: two coaxial unit squares (cap A z=0 face 0, cap B z=1 face 1) — the SAME setup as tests/test_bridge.d's loadCaps(). Segments law (spans=max(1,segments); interior rings linearly interpolated at t=i/spans) and Twist law for |twist|<=1 (f(t)=smoothstep(t)=3t^2-2t^3, slide toward the next/previous ring corner) are both closed-form and reference-VERIFIED (task 0357's private capture doc) — expected_vertices below are hand-derived from those closed forms on this exact fixture, not borrowed byte-for-byte from any external capture (the reference capture's own base case used a degenerate closed-cube selection unsuitable for a clean vertex-position golden).
source: closed-form (task 0357 verified Segments/Twist laws) applied to a hand-authored fixture; see per-case description

provenance.notes: Closed-form Segments/Twist laws verified in task 0357, applied to a hand-authored base fixture; see per-case description in the fixture itself.

cases.name: polygon_default_remove_true
cases.description: Segments=1 (default), Twist=0, Remove Polygons=true — the toolcard's parity law: no interior rings, both caps deleted, 4 lateral bridge quads remain. No new vertices at spans=1.

cases.name: polygon_remove_false
cases.description: Remove Polygons=false — both original caps PRESERVED alongside the 4 new bridge quads (bit-exact opposite of the default-true case above, task 0357 requirement).

cases.name: polygon_segments3
cases.description: Segments law, Twist=0: spans=3 -> 2 interior rings at t=1/3, 2/3, linearly interpolated between the paired loop corners (identity pairing on this fixture). Closed-form, exact.

cases.name: polygon_twist1_integer_exact
cases.description: Twist law, |twist|=1 — the VERIFIED-EXACT integer regime (task 0357 findings_twist_dense.md: max error ~3e-8 at every interior ring, two independent reference loop shapes). f(1/3)=7/27, f(2/3)=20/27 (smoothstep), each interior-ring vertex slides toward the NEXT ring corner by that fraction.

cases.name: polygon_huge_segments_dos_regression
cases.description: DoS regression (task 0357 review convention): a huge Segments value must clamp, not hang/allocate unbounded memory. Param.enforceBounds() clamps at the UI layer (max 64); the kernel's own maxBridgeSpans=512 defense-in-depth cap is exercised directly by mesh.d's module unittest (this HTTP-level case pins the end-to-end clamp to the PARAM's own max=64, which is the value actually reachable through tool.attr / this command params path).
